data.epi_content:
Everyone must be laughing at me.

Look at that pathetic lady, she ran away without saying a word to that insect-like Black!

Rather than running away... did I actually run?

I don't know.

As all kinds of confusion welled up, I felt my face burning with shame.

I'm not in a position to feel embarrassed at all, but this feeling might be because I'm sad.

When I was bullied and humiliated, no one even glanced at me or cared about me, but when I tried to deal with that ignorant, lowly woman, everyone condemned me, and even that filthy beast spoke and drove me away.

With those unclean hands, he dared, DARED to push me.

And I couldn't say anything.

Because the gazes of others were burdensome, because I was afraid of the sword held by a mere slave.

Well, this is how I've always lived.

When I was locked in a locker, begging to be let out while curled up in that dark place unable to stretch my legs, some laughed at me and giggled, and Lydia further encouraged that atmosphere.

She must have changed her bullying tactics this time, using her body to bring me such shame.

She must have known that disgusting Black was walking this way.

Nothing has really changed.

If my profession had been a prostitute, I would have just laughed it off with a smile.

They are hardworking laborers who sell their laughter, bodies, and most importantly, their minds.

But since I'm not quite a prostitute who sells her body, I was angry and struggled, trying desperately to escape this situation.

And then, my goodness.

If I hadn't had a gun, if I hadn't fired it at the doorway, I would have been bullied by Lydia and had my self-esteem torn to shreds.

"No one helped me, but when I tried to do something, everyone started getting in my way."

My facial muscles, which had been calm and expressionless until just now, began to distort wildly and tremble, either from emotion or some disability developing in me.

I wiped my face once with my palm and started taking deep breaths.

I begin pouring warm water into a cup on the table in the middle of the room, which should help calm my mind.

Steam begins to rise.

I sprinkle finely ground tea powder on top, and when the color has steeped sufficiently, I drink it in one gulp.

Then, with a somewhat familiar feeling, I take the cup and smash it.

Immediately follows the sound of glass weakly splitting in half.

I pick up a small piece of the broken glass, stare at my arm for a long time, and eventually shake my head.

I just throw the piece I was holding far away.

Going out to the balcony, I hear a clinking sound after a while, indicating it had fallen to the ground.

Judging by the brief scream I heard, someone must have been passing by.

Finding it oddly funny, I let out a hollow laugh.

Then I just sit down.

I accidentally put my hand where glass fragments remained.

Since the pieces were so large, they didn't pierce my hand but just slightly scratched the skin before sliding away.

I pick up the remaining glass pieces and throw them off the balcony as well.

This time, instead of going outside the building as I wanted, they just shattered on the balcony with a clinking sound.

"Nothing works out."

With slightly trembling hands, I light the tobacco I had just rubbed against Lydia, which was in my pocket, and begin to inhale the smoke.
data.epi_content2:
It's all my fault.

The problem is that I'm still alive.

My breathing and living day by day must be truly displeasing to those looking down from above.

I haven't even done anything to deserve such hatred.

There's a slightly bitter taste in my mouth.

I want something sweet.

Checking the clock, I see about half an hour has passed.

I take one of the two remaining gold coins from the drawer and leave the room again.

Opening the door with two bullet holes, what I see outside is the blood and filth that Lydia must have spilled, still not cleaned up.

I frown at the unpleasant smell and appearance, silently blame the lazy maid, and click my tongue once.

And on the right, as I come out, several people look at me and murmur.

They must be idle people who came to watch after hearing about the fight that happened here.

Some faces are even familiar.

I don't know what they're saying, but judging by how they look at me with vile expressions, laughing and chattering, I could guess it wasn't anything good.

Well, people who couldn't even make eye contact with me before can now point fingers and criticize me, and I can't say anything about it.

I thought I was used to it, but it feels new every time I experience it.

The son of a weapons merchant who supplies sharp-edged shovels useful for various purposes, wrist cutters, magic-imbued swords, and various firearms while preaching peace.

The daughter of a lord who brings people from the eastern kingdom, puts women in brothels, uses men as tenant farmers or laborers, yet considers them inferior.

A person who doesn't even know their father's profession while benefiting greatly from the public brothel system, yet views prostitutes with disgust.

A student who follows extreme theology claiming that magicians should all be burned to death as heretics, even though their family's power must have risen from magic (I heard their father lamenting this).

The daughter of a worm-like lord who claims Blacks are humans like us, yet exploits the people on her own territory worse than dogs.

They're all uncivilized and stupid compared to the House of Mecklenburg.

Rather than ordering all nobles to bring their children here, the Emperor, whoever they might be, should have cut down all the nobles and ruled all territories directly.

I didn't like this contradictory system that was both centralized and feudal.

These stupid people, who are merely nobles like myself, try to speak to me as if they're my equals, which is quite presumptuous.

Well, saying such things has no meaning now.

My foundation has long since crumbled.

They look at me and giggle, mocking my downfall while praising their own morality.

Such inadequate humans are pointing fingers at me and tearing me down.

Insects who don't even know what kind of humans they are are pointing fingers at me!

My hands start trembling, but I didn't recklessly draw my gun and start shooting.

Such actions are for weak humans who have lost their minds in shell holes.

"Yes, just until Vivian comes. Just until Vivian comes."

I mutter this while glaring at them for a while, then turn away.

Even though they're quite far away, I can hear them laughing at me.

Maybe I should assume I'm crazy and try to escape from the Academy and the Capital.

There are four gates in the north, south, east, and west, and soldiers block all entrances and exits.

There's no way I could escape.

If I went to a back alley and offered what little money I have to be smuggled out, they would probably take my money and throw me into a brothel.

They'd cut my heels so I couldn't run away.

A prostitute doesn't need legs to walk around anyway.
data.epi_content3:
With this face, I can't even think about going somewhere to work.

Standing next to Vivian might make me look a bit faded, but I have a beautiful, dignified face that clearly shows I've been well-fed and well-rested.

I thought I had already shaken off these thoughts, but apparently not.

Well, what wouldn't I do to survive?

It's just that my range of thinking only goes this far.

I keep thinking of the same things, and when I come to the store like now, I buy the same things.

And the fact that I forget the Crown Prince often loiters around this store, smoking tobacco and buying goods, and then meet him again...

I can only think that my range of thinking is very narrow.

In other words, I'm incredibly stupid.

And as always, the Crown Prince approaches me.

"You caused quite a commotion just now."

He stands in front of the counter and throws money to the clerk.

Then he picks up a box of tobacco from the end of the store and hands it to me.

Knowing he wouldn't accept a refusal, I silently took what he offered and tried to leave.

Then something grabs my shoulder.

When I turn around, it's not the Crown Prince but the shadow beneath him that was holding me.

"Let's move somewhere else."

He speaks as if he won't accept any objections, and we often talk... no, that's not right.

It's just me who does that.

Anyway, he kidnapped me to the pond where the carp are.

I tried not to move a single step, but considering the shadow dragged me here, "kidnapped" seems like the right word.

"Aren't you going to smoke?"

He points to the tobacco in my hand.

I frown once, open the box, and put the tobacco in my mouth.

After lighting it, I inhale the smoke.

"At your age, acting so recklessly could cut your lifeline at any moment. Wouldn't it be better to show some restraint?"

"Well, I feel like my line has been cut many times already. Seems I have quite a lot of lines."

He must have thought I was talking nonsense, as he just laughed it off.

"Well, anyway, you seem to have grown up quite recklessly, but remember this.

In this prosperous imperial capital, many have suffered at the hands of vicious groups in back alleys."

I tried to draw my gun after hearing this nonsense.

"You should also be careful with your hands."

At the same time, my hand fell to the floor, just like what happened last time.

It wasn't cut off or anything like that. It just dropped to the floor.

How should I put it? Just...

I felt powerless.
